message.content:
<deliberation>
Step-by-step plan:
1) For each main REG node (REG-092, REG-093, REG-095, REG-021, REG-013), compare the policy chunk against the GDPR text provided.
2) Identify and list any explicit cross-references the Article makes and include the relevant provided referenced_nodes in "considered_references".
3) Detect any deviations (differences in constraints, details, or scope). If missing/omitted content, classify as Non-Deviation per instruction 4.
4) Where a deviation exists, extract exact GDPR and policy quotes that substantiate it, classify using the taxonomy, and provide a concise mismatch sentence and analysis.
5) Produce one JSON object per REG id, marking deviation_found true/false with deviations array accordingly.

Evaluations:
- REG-092 (Art 77(1)): Policy states the right to lodge a complaint “if you believe ... infringes data protection law,” whereas GDPR states “infringes this Regulation.” This broadens the trigger beyond GDPR to any “data protection law.” This is a deviation in scope. Classify as Data deviation (scope altered).
- REG-093 (Art 77(2)): The Article requires SA to inform complainant on progress/outcome incl. possibility of judicial remedy under Art 78. The policy chunk is silent on this. Treat as Non-Deviation (omission). Include Art 78 references (REG-094, REG-095, REG-096, REG-097) in considered_references.
- REG-095 (Art 78(2)): Establishes right to effective judicial remedy if SA does not handle complaint or fails to inform within 3 months. Policy chunk does not discuss this. Non-Deviation (omission). Include cross-refs to Art 77 (REG-092, REG-093) as the Article references 77; Articles 55 and 56 are not provided.
- REG-021 (Art 15(1)): Right of access details. Policy chunk does not include these specifics. Non-Deviation (omission). Include cross-reference to Art 22(1) (REG-041) since 15(1)(h) references it.
- REG-013 (Art 13(2)): Requires multiple disclosures. Policy chunk addresses 13(2)(e) around whether provision of data is statutory/contractual/necessary and consequences, with examples (website use, contact form, orders). No contradiction detected. Other elements (a)-(d),(f) not present—treated as omissions. Include cross-refs to 13(1) (REG-012), 6(1) (REG-001), 9(2) (REG-009), and 22(1) (REG-041).

All GDPR and policy quotes are taken exactly from the provided nodes and chunk.
</deliberation>
```json
[
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-092",
    "clause": "Art77(1)",
    "considered_references": [],
    "deviation_found": true,
    "deviations": [
      {
        "deviation_type": "Data deviation",
        "gdpr_quote": "every data subject shall have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the Member State of his or her habitual residence, place of work or place of the alleged infringement if the data subject considers that the processing of personal data relating to him or her infringes this Regulation.",
        "policy_quote": "In accordance with Art 77 GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority if you believe that the processing of your personal data infringes data protection law.",
        "mismatch": "The policy expands the trigger from infringements of “this Regulation” to infringements of broader “data protection law.”",
        "analysis": "GDPR Art 77(1) specifies the complaint right applies where processing infringes the GDPR itself; the policy broadens this condition to any data protection law, altering the scope of when a complaint may be lodged."
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-093",
    "clause": "Art77(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-095", "REG-094", "REG-096", "REG-097"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-095",
    "clause": "Art78(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-092", "REG-093"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-021",
    "clause": "Art15(1)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  },
  {
    "reg_id": "REG-013",
    "clause": "Art13(2)",
    "considered_references": ["REG-012", "REG-001", "REG-009", "REG-041"],
    "deviation_found": false,
    "deviations": []
  }
]
```
